Further, there were still noted unpaid labor payrolls for completed 20% DF projects as of 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned contractor to refund the cost deficiency of ₱87,624.60 in the Construction of Slaughter House and the excess of contract amount above the COA estimates by a total of ₱125,132.79 due to misapplication of Indirect Cost and VAT.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to submit justification on the noted deficiencies.
observations.reason: The validity and reasonableness of the justification is yet to be evaluated through the assistance of TAS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor to rectify the noted deficiencies or refund the total cost deficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to submit justification on the noted deficiencies.
observations.reason: The validity and reasonableness of the justification is yet to be evaluated through the assistance of TAS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the HRMO require the employees without ATM cards to submit their application forms to the DBP as a requirement for subsequent issuance of ATM Cards to them;

the Management consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel and coordinate with the DBP officials and employees for the processing of the same; and

see to it that the cash advance granted shall not exceed the Maximum Cash Accountability authorized under her approved fidelity bond or else apply for an increased fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Upon hiring of Job Order personnel, the Municipality requires accomplishment of ATM application forms for their payroll. Cash advance for payroll of JO is only prepared if ATMs applied are still not issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized the fund by trying their best to locate for suitable land as dumpsite and observe the criteria for sanitary landfill as required by Section 40 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipality, additional appropriation was already allotted and POW was already prepared. The Municipality is already on push through for the construction of Phase I for a cost of ₱6.5 million.
observations.reason: Allotted funds were not utilized due to limited information on the exact reference points, coordinates and ground elevation of the proposed location.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Assessor account for the additional twelve (12) land titles in custody of the Municipal Treasurer which were not included in the Municipal assessor’s records;

The Municipal Assessor, General Services Officer, and Treasurer coordinate and exert efforts in obtaining documents necessary for titling the parcels of land accounted in the name of the Municipality; and the

Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO and OIC-Municipal Assessor was able to submit a list of properties owned by the Municipality which disclosed that eleven (11) parcels of land were still untitled as of December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Twelve (12) parcels of land remained untitled  based on the records of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer, Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant submit valid justification on the utilization
of BGCM in the procurement of medicines and medical/dental supplies not
directly related in the treatment and/or prevention of COVID-19.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC, ensure
that all procurement projects of the Municipality regardless of the modality are
properly included in the approved APP and that all changes thereto are covered
by supplemental/revised APP. We further recommended that the Municipal
Mayor, BAC and end-user unit strictly comply with the revised procedures of
the Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases) modality prescribed in GPPB
Resolution No. 03-2020 dated March 9, 2020.

We recommended that the BAC, end-user and other concerned officials:
a. continue to require updated file of its supplier to facilitate identification of
legally, technically, and financially capable supplier and contractor in case
of alternative modes of procurement especially during emergency;
b. require submission of correct and appropriate OSS which contains
complete declarations/commitments as prescribed in RA 9184 and other
GPPB issuances, as applicable, for every procurement project that
requires the same;
c. clearly indicate in the RFQ/Proposal at what stage the documentary
requirements shall be submitted when alternative mode of procurement is
used;
d. ensure that NOA, PO/Contract and NTP, if necessary, are posted in
PhilGEPS/GPPB Online Portal or any other online platforms that may be
required for the purpose and likewise attach any proof of posting of the
same to the LGU’s website and conspicuous places within the
Municipality; and
e. explain the absence of target date of completion/implementation in the
POWs/PR of the subject procurement projects and provide justification
on the grant of 60-day extension in the delivery of supplies, furniture and
fixtures considering that the procurement was based on urgent situations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a written
justification on failure to issue Official Receipt for the cash donations received
which led to its non-recognition to the books. Henceforth, we recommended the
strict compliance with existing guidelines in handling cash and in-kind
donations received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the MDRRMO
and BAC, submit valid justification on the utilization of QRF for the subject
contract awarded prior to the declaration of state of calamity. Henceforth,
validate first with the appropriate office the availability of funds prior to the
approval of APP and award of contract for any procurement projects subject to
Negotiated Procurement.

We recommended that the Local School Board consider the inclusion in
the SEF budget the funding of programs, projects and activities that would
support the alternative learning modalities, digital education, digital
infrastructure, and continuity plans of the schools within the Municipality
amidst the COVID-19 pandemic and ensure optimal utilization and timely
implementation of such PPAs.

We recommended that the MGSO submit all the lacking documents and
other reports to establish fuel consumption of COVID-19 frontliners and
responders and ensure that similar transactions be supported with proper and
complete documentary requirements as required in COA Circular No. 77-61
dated September 26, 1977.

We recommended that the:
a. Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count of all the
properties and prepare properly the (i) RPCPPE indicating each
property item rather than lump sum balances per account to establish
the actual quantity counted and (ii) RPCLRN presenting the local road
network by road and its component;
b. MGSO prepare and maintain appropriate property cards for all the
properties, including all roads and its components;
c. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and MGSO with
the complete description and cost segregation of road components
especially those that were consolidated in RLRN without the necessary
details and description;
d. MGSO, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant exert efforts in
the reconciliation of RLRN and the accounting records; and
e. Municipal Accountant (i) include in the Notes to Financial Statements
the required disclosure for Road Networks, and (ii) coordinate with
Municipal Treasurer, MGSO and Municipal Assessor in gathering
necessary documents relative to the unrecorded parcels of land and
recognize the same in the books at cost or fair value as appropriate.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) prepare necessary
adjusting entries to revert back the cost of on-going project to CIP-Building
and Other Infrastructure account and to derecognize the corresponding
depreciation improperly recorded; (b) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer
in verifying and evaluating the actual status of the projects under CIP accounts
and in validating whether these projects were already completed and brought
to its intended purpose prior to its reclassification to appropriate asset account;
and (c) recognize the cost of materials delivered for stock and later issuance for
the construction projects to Construction Materials Inventory account and
credit the same only upon issuance, transfers or other disposal.

We further recommended that the Municipal Engineer regularly
prepare and submit to Municipal Accountant status of projects and the
issuance of construction materials for proper accounting.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer prepare the list of unclaimed checks at the end of
the year and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for
preparation of JEV to restore the amount of unreleased checks to Cash-
in-Bank and recognize the appropriate liability account every end of
year, and reverse the same in the ensuing year;
b. Municipal Accountant draw necessary adjustments for the noted stale
checks following the procedures prescribed under Section 59, Volume I
of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Treasurer reconcile their records and settle
any disparity in the balances of cash accounts per General Ledger and
cashbook.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. exert all efforts, thru the Municipal Accountant, to locate Memorandum
of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement or any other documents
supporting the long outstanding balance of Due to NGAs to determine
the Source Agency, the purpose for which the funds were transferred,
status of program/project implementation, and other relevant terms and
conditions; and
b. implement the intended projects and programs, if warranted, and
properly liquidate the amount in accordance with the terms and
conditions governing the transferred funds; otherwise, cause the return
of the balance to the Source Agency or remit to the BTr if so stipulated.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Chairperson, thru the BAC Technical Working Group and
Secretariat, provide valid explanation on the noted lapses specifically on
the delays in the procurement process, inconsistencies in the dates
indicated in various documents and the absence of necessary
procurement documents;
b. BAC strictly observe the period of action provided in RA 9184 and its
IRR within which certain stages of the procurement process must be
completed;
c. BAC and Municipal Engineer properly evaluate and ensure consistency
in the dates indicated in all the relevant documents submitted pertaining
to procurement and implementation of contracts, especially the start and
completion date, for proper and accurate evaluation of contract
implementation; and
d. Municipal Accountant, BAC, and Municipal Engineer ensure that future
claims be supported with the documents as prescribed under COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the:
a. MPDC, MDRRMC and MDRRM Officer provide the specific and
detailed PPAs in the LDRRMFIP to facilitate implementation of the
disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness activities and to avoid
the use of fund of these PPAs to augment the insufficiency of funds of
some PPAs without undergoing proper budgeting procedures. Likewise,
the MDRRM Office ensure that the amount appropriated for the PPAs
shall be the ceiling amount in the preparation of the project design;
b. MDRRMC use the prescribed form shown in Annex A of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in preparing the Annual and
Supplemental/Revised LDRRMFIP;
c. MDRRMC revisit the conditions on the grant of financial assistance by
LBP specifically the return of unutilized amount, if any, and coordinate
79
with the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant for the
immediate return of the ₱25,000.00 to the LBP, if stipulated, or for its
remittance to the BTr;
d. MDRRMC prepare Revised/Supplemental LDRRMFIP whenever there
are new PPAs that will be implemented out of the MDRRMF and to
observe strict compliance with Section 6.2 of the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013; and
e. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their
records pertaining to the utilization of MDRRMF before transferring
the unexpended balance to Special Trust Fund at the end of the year to
ensure that all the unutilized balance of QRF and 70% MF- MOOE of
MDRRMF will be transferred correctly.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRM Officer, MGSO and the Municipal Accountant submit written
explanation on the procurement of diesel more than the project
requirement which was equivalent to ₱186,832.00 payment out of the
MDRRMF;
b. BAC and MGSO ensure that the quantity of fuel being procured
matches the project requirements per POW and PR;
c. BAC justify the existence and the veracity of the ground relied for the
use of Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases) for the procurement
of modular tent; otherwise, explain how the use of such modality, instead
of competitive bidding, has not been detrimental to the government;
d. BAC and end-user or procuring unit comply with the requirements of
GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020 in case Negotiated Procurement
(Emergency Procurement) will be resorted to as procurement modality;
e. Municipal Treasurer secure Acknowledgment Receipt from the two
recipients of cash assistance claimed to have not signed the original
distribution list as a substantial evidence that they had received their
claims and henceforth, require the submission of authority from the
claimant and identification documents whenever payments are made to
individual other that the payee; and
f. MDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant refrain from paying
expenses through cash advance if it is practical to pay the
supplier/contractor directly by check.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC (i) send invitations to observers at least five (5) calendar days
before the date of the procurement stage/activity, (ii) notify the losing
bidder within three (3) days from the issuance of the resolution
recommending award of the contract, (iii) post the NOA in the
PhilGEPS within three (3) calendar days from its issuance and in any
conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality, (iv) ensure that
the date of receipt of NOA and NTP were specified, and (v) use the latest
GPPB-prescribed PBD, all in compliance to the 2016 Revised IRR of RA
9184;
b. TWG carefully examine the documents submitted by the Lowest
Calculated Bidder during post-qualification in compliance to Section
34.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184;
c. Municipal Accountant ensure that advance payment to the winning
bidder is supported with an irrevocable letter of credit, bank guarantee
or a surety bond callable upon demand as required under Item 4.2 in
Annex E of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
d. Henceforth, the Municipal Mayor, through concerned officials, ensure
compliance with the guidelines covering the utilization of the funds
received from DILG and other National Government Agencies, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal
Accountant and other concerned officials to:
a. strictly monitor officers and employees with outstanding cash advances
and refrain from granting additional cash advance to an employee prior
to settlement of his/her previous cash advances;
b. grant cash advance for travelling expenses directly to the officers and
employees who will be travelling to establish accountability;
c. consider designating other bonded employees as Special Disbursing
Officers to cash advance for special purpose/undertaking, whenever
necessary, to avoid overlapping cash advances;
d. ensure that liquidation of the accountable officers are within the
reglementary period timely in compliance to the prescribed guidelines
COA Circular Nos. 97-002, 2012-001 and 2012-004, as applicable;
e. pay by check directly to the supplier whenever practicable in accordance
with the general principle of imprest system; and
f. ensure that the utilization of cash advances were supported by proper
and sufficient supporting documents.

We recommended the MGSO to prepare the PIF listing all insurable
assets, properties and interests of the Municipality following the guidelines and
format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 and
submit the same to GSIS, copy furnished the Audit Team, on or before April 30
of each year as basis for the assessment of general insurance coverage.

We reiterated our recommendation that the:
a. Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal
Planning and Development Officer in the planning stage (i) consider the
urgency of the project and the climate or weather factor in stating the
period of implementation of the PPAs; and (ii) adopt a mode of
implementation that will fast track the completion of their programmed
developmental projects especially those that are in its preparatory
stages;
b. The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review
and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed
projects and those already identified as non-priority projects as well as
those already funded and implemented by other government agency for
reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322
of RA 7160.
We also recommended that the:
c. Municipal Engineer (i) formulate strategic plan that will aid in the
effective and efficient coordination, monitoring, reporting and
evaluation of the status of implementation of the PPAs for the optimal
utilization of 20% Development Fund; (ii) review and analyze the
occurrence of unpaid labor cost for the implemented projects; (iii)
include information relative the targeted project duration in the
preparation of program of works for PPAs especially those to be
implemented through by administration as basis for proper planning,
monitoring and evaluation; and (iv) together with the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer and General Services Officer provide
status report of the materials procured but were not yet used as of year-
end, including the whereabouts and its actual condition and photo
documentation.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Point System, and other offices
concerned:
a. ensure that, in the preparation of GPB, GAD objectives are SMART
(specific, measurable, attainable, realistic and time-bound), reflect the
results that the Municipality intends to achieve, and not articulated as
an activity statement;
b. comply with RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and other existing
regulations in the implementation of the PPAs included in the GPB;
c. consistently observe the guide in completing the GAD AR prescribed
under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2016-01 especially in
accomplishing Columns 7, 8, 12 and 14;
d. exhaust all available resources for the collection and processing of
complete and reliable data requirements of the GAD Database to be
used in data processing and gender analysis for gender-responsive
planning and budgeting;
e. include priority gender issues in the succeeding GPB that may be
identified through analysis of the information in the GAD database as
well as the corresponding PPAs that will address the same;
f. prepare the GAD Agenda through proper consultations with local
functionaries and stakeholders to ensure that priority gender issues will
be addressed within the timeframe; and
g. make representation with the Sangguniang Bayan to consider amending
its existing GAD Code to include provision for the development of IRR
indicating among others the groups and entities which shall promulgate
the same.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality, through its Inventory Committee, conducted physical count of properties, plant and equipment and inventory items and furnished the Audit team copies of their reports. Continuous coordination with other Department Heads is also being conducted to properly classify its PPE items. The full accomplishment of RPCPPE shall be dealt with after the posting of stickers to previously unrecorded PPE as per actual inventory taking and reconciliation.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 pandemic restrictions, the work from home scheme defers full accomplishment of the required reports. Also, Property cards for PPE and road networks is yet to be accomplished as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer verify and evaluate the actual status of the projects recorded under CIP accounts and validate whether these projects were already completed or should be retained under the CIP accounts; and (b) Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry for the proper recording and reporting of PPE accounts, and exert extra effort in identifying details of the long outstanding balance recorded under the CIP account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the adjusting entries for the noted CIP accounts. Monitoring of completed projects were already coordinated with the Municipal Engineer to prevent recurrence of the noted finding.
observations.reason: For the noted outstanding lump sum amount of ₱5.187 million, a total amount of ₱2.18 million only (4 projects) was transferred to appropriate asset accounts. Details of specific projects and locations are still not yet provided due to unavailability of records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Property and Disposal Committee (a) coordinate and prepare supporting schedules and records to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the inventory accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the inventory; (b) prepare the RPCI per account and not on a per office basis; (c) conduct regular reconciliation between the Accounting and GSO records for inventory accounts and effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (d) prepare and maintain appropriate inventory records and reports for each Inventory account.
observations.managementAction: Accounting for the Inventories were monitored and reconciled with records of the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO). Inventory records were also maintained both the Municipal Accountant and MGSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

Municipal Agriculturist to closely coordinate with the Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant in the inventory and reconciliation of the discrepancy noted, and periodically provide the Municipal Accounting Office the inventory of breeding stocks maintained by the MAgO for recording purposes;

Municipal Accountant be guided by PPSAS 27 – Agriculture on the proper recording and disclosure of breeding stocks maintained by the Municipality; and

Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist establish and maintain WOABSLC and WOABSPC, respectively, which shall contain the details of the breeding stocks account as prescribed in Section 114 of Manual on the NGAS  for LGU, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted physical count of the biological assets and reconciled its balances with the Accounting records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO to analyze the supporting documents relative to the premiums paid for the insurance of the Municipal properties and effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the corresponding prepaid insurance in the books of the Municipality. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant ensure observance of accrual basis method of accounting in recording the Municipal transactions pursuant to PPSAS 1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the journal entry vouchers for the adjustment of the insurance expenses noted. Accounting for Prepaid insurance is already applied for the insurance procurement of properties and motor vehicles owned by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality, through the Local Finance Committee (a) provide explanation on the Municipality’s decision to avail loans instead of using its available resources; (b) make use of the cost-benefit analysis pertaining to scarce resources of the government to secure the most advantageous alternative for the government; and (c) carefully evaluate its cash position before obtaining credit financing to ensure efficiency of financial management.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality admitted that there was no cost-benefit analysis done in the contract of loan but provided justifications on the steps taken for the loan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and the HRMO institute effective controls and monitoring of the payrolls particularly the deductions of the Municipal employees of Naujan to ensure that net take home pay shall not be less than the minimum allowed as provided under the Annual GAA.
observations.managementAction: The municipality crafted a loan approval system wherein payslips issued every cut-off shall be used as reference, instead of certification of balance of salary, to monitor the Net Take Home Pay still available prior approval of loan application.
observations.reason: Out of 25 employees in the previous year, only 5 employees remained to have below the ₱5,000 net take home pay. Subject employees shall not be allowed to renew loans for them to catch up with the required net take home pay.

observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairperson is implementing measures to prevent delays in posting of NOA and NTP, and tasked CWG to conduct examination of documents prior and during bidding and post-evaluation to avoid incorrect details in the documents. Also, the Municipality abided with the Revised IRR of RA 9184 with regards to the treatment of 15% mobilization for new projects. Further, Statement of Time Elapsed (STE) formed part of the documentary requirements for every progress billing.
observations.reason: Delays in posting of NOA and NTP in PhilGEPS, discrepancies of details in documents, and absence of prescribed security for 15% mobilization were still noted as findings in audit of CY 2020 transactions.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality provided justification and explanations on the noted findings and submitted various documents to prove compliance the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-3 dated May 23, 2017, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2017-160 dated November 29, 2017, and DBM Local Budget Circular No. 117 dated February 5,2018. The Municipal Mayor assured that submission of complete documents for their projects will be properly observed in their future transactions. The treatment of the construction transactions with regard to Trust Fund account will likewise be observed in accordance with COA Circular No.  2015-009 dated December 1, 2015. The Municipality will also religiously scrutinize the documents, particularly the dates to avoid doubts on the validity and accuracy of the documents.
observations.reason: Reason for suspension of work provided in the reply dated February 28, 2020 were not among the reasons provided for in Section 9.2 of Annex E of Revised IRR of RA 9184. Also, delayed posting of NOA in PhilGEPS and submission of incomplete documents were still noted in CY 2020 Audit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

require the Municipal Engineer to submit (i) explanation on the noted differences between the reported and COA verified accomplishments for the above-cited projects and inconsistencies in the naming of the project Construction of Manpower Development Center at Brgy. Santiago; (ii) copies of the approved suspension/resume order, if any, otherwise through the help of the Municipal Accountant compute for the amount of liquidated damages brought by the delay of the contractor in the completion of the Establishment of OTOP Express Store and Construction of Evacuation Center at Brgy. Melgar; and (iii) lacking documents such as Quality Control Programs including all Material Test Reports for the projects listed above as items c and e for further review and evaluation of the contract; 

make a formal demand against the contractors/suppliers to make the necessary corrective measures for the noted defects and non-conformance with specification and non-observance during the site inspection of item SPL-II (Road shoulder) specified as “Concrete” in Detailed estimates per contract or pay for the cost deficiencies totaling ₱544,044.20; and 

require the Municipal GSO and members of the Inspectorate Team to make an explanation relative the noted observations on the Supply and Delivery of the three (3) units reconditioned heavy equipment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted explanation with supporting documents for the noted issues.
observations.reason: Documents were forwarded to TAS for evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

Local Development Council, Implementing Office and Municipal Planning and Development Officer in the planning stage (i) consider various factors, such as urgency of the project and the climate or weather factor in stating the period of implementation of the PPAs; (ii) adopt a mode of implementation that will fast track the completion of their programmed developmental projects especially those that are in its preparatory stages; and (iii) evaluate the viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional fund needed to complete a project programmed in the previous year;

Municipal Engineer and the concerned implementing department heads: (i) periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the MGN as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, (ii) submit a list of all completed projects to support the reprogramming to other developmental projects or reversion to the unappropriated surplus; and (iii) specify the planned implementation period of projects under the Annual Investment Plan as a guide for the effective implementation of the projects; and

Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: According to concerned offices, project status were properly reviewed and monitored and reports were promptly submitted for verification.
observations.reason:
On-going implementation of audit recommendations. 

Existence of projects which are (a) still in the preparatory stage of project implementation; (b) still waiting for the completion of previously implemented projects; (c) funded and implemented by other agencies; and (d) non-priority (for reversion) are among the reasons noted for failure to optimally utilize 20% DF.

Also, lack of technical staff needed to prepare the pre-engineering design and Program of Works as well as the influx of projects from seventy (70) barangays, which likewise require technical assistance, hinder the timely implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality implement the remedies/options as provided for in LBC No. 100 dated October 1, 2012 which includes adoption of cost-cutting measures to achieve desirable outcomes envisioned by the operations of Bahay Tuklasan.
observations.managementAction: A P5M appropriation already bided to complete the facility for full operation. There is an ongoing revision of revenue charges for municipal facilities. Hearing already started on January 21, 2021.
observations.reason: Due to COVID 19 pandemic, the Bahay Tuklasan was not able to generate revenues in 2020.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipality submitted its GAD Agenda and ensure that the recommendations will be applied. Further, the GAD key personnel has only attended one (1) seminar/training in relation to GAD in 2020 prior to lockdown. 

GAD continuously reviews their GAD Code for updating.

The Municipality scheduled a training on March 8-9, 2021 for the identification of Gender Issues and Concern in accordance with the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Due to insufficient understanding in the preparation of IRR of GAD Code, GAD database, and GAD Agenda, preparation of these documents were either not performed or found deficient. 

Attendance to training was not prioritized due to restrictions and limitations brought by pandemic. Further, the budget for such trainings were realigned to support projects against COVID-19.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Engineer make a justifiable explanation on the noted deficiency between the reported and the actual accomplishment of the project for the Construction of Three (3) Storey Evacuation Center. 

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor make a formal demand against the Althaea Enterprises to perform corrective measures or refund the cost deficiency ₱139,766.37 attributed to the noted difference of 1.17% for the construction of evacuation center and the noted cracks and holes in the Riprapping of Tabang Creek.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the:
GSO should obtain from the Accounting Office the listing of properties per category of PPE and make use of the property numbers and responsibility centers stated therein as reference in the inventory taking by the Inventory Committee;

Municipal Accountant and GSO coordinate and prepare supporting schedules to facilitate the proper reconciliation of the property accounts and establishment of actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties; and

Municipal Accountant to effect proper adjustments in the books for items that necessitate corrections as identified in the verification and reconciliation process, create separate subsidiary ledgers for the unreconciled account balances and observe proper disclosure of the same in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation of accounts.

We recommended that the:

Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the local road network to determine the current value of the road components to facilitate proper preparation of RPCLRN;

Municipal Engineer prepare a schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component such as (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope protection structures, (d) and other miscellaneous structures to be submitted to the GSO and Accounting Office. Moreover, subsequent road projects of the Municipality should clearly indicate the separate cost of each road component for easy reference.

The Engineering Office is also vested to determine the life of the Local Roads taking into consideration the (1) design, (2) type of users, and (3) volume of users of the roads. Such detail should also be included in the schedule of Local Roads to serve basis for the depreciation of the Road Components to be applied by the Accounting Office;

GSO prepare and maintain RLRN and RoadMap to account for the Local Roads. These records should be reconciled with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office; and

Municipal Accountant prepare separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the determined cost of the Road Network Components, and thereafter provide for the depreciation of each Road Component based from the records to be prepared by the Engineering Office to establish proper valuation of the Road Network.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that
the Inventory Committee, in the course of a complete physical inventory of properties, account all determined unserviceable properties including the aforesaid Heavy Equipment to facilitate preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and other required supporting documentations for disposal. Thereafter, the Committee determine the salvage value of the properties based on property ledgers maintained by the Accounting Office, appraise the disposal value of the unserviceable properties through technical assessment by the Engineering Office, and dispose them pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality, thru its Inventory Committee, conducted physical count of properties, plant and equipment and inventory items and furnished the Audit team copies of their reports. Continuous coordination with other Department Heads is also being conducted to properly classify its PPE items. The preparation of RPCPPE shall be dealt with after the posting of stickers to previously unrecorded PPE as per actual inventory taking and reconciliation.
observations.reason: Due to COVID-19 Enhanced Community Quarantine, the work from home scheme defers full accomplishment of the required reports and the process of coordinating with different departments. Ledger cards for the property, plant and equipment and road networks remained unaccomplished as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the:

Municipal Treasurer (i) formulate strategies to intensify collection of property taxes and raise awareness of tax payers, including individual postings of the notice of property tax dues, current and delinquent, to each of the seventy (70) Barangays of the Municipality thru the cooperation of the ABC President and the Barangay Officials; (ii) establish monitoring tool for the delinquent accounts which will aid in the easy generation of the report with information as to the aging of the delinquencies since the same cannot be generated using the iTax system; and (iii) exert additional effort to serve Notice of Delinquencies and Tax Due Bills to taxpayers with delinquent accounts in all the 70 barangays through the assistance of the ABC President, Punong Barangays and other Barangay officials.

Municipal Assessor reconcile their data with the list provided by the MTO regarding properties suggested for restriction and/or exclusion; and require the Field Assessment and Appraisal Personnel to conduct regular tax mapping and property reassessment.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office is conducting continuous Information and Education Campaign (IEC) to the barangays on property tax and tax amnesty in coordination with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office and Barangay Officials despite the hindrances of the pandemic. The Municipality issued
23,610 Tax Due Bills 
as of December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: According to the Municipality, five (5) seaside barangays were not served with Notice of Delinquency due to hindrance of COVID-19 pandemic in disseminating to said barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the:

Municipal Accountant observed proper accounting procedure in recording the disbursements out of Trust Liability-DRRM and even out of continuing appropriations and in proper recognition of liability of accounts and draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the transactions for the fair presentation of the affected accounts in the Financial Statements; 
Concerned official and employees submit the necessary documents;
Members of the Inspection Committee and GSO properly accomplished the IAR by indicating the dates for the efficient and effective evaluation of the documents and transactions; and 
Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the MDRRM Council properly plan, prioritize and evaluate the PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF to attain the objective and purpose of the Fund by observing the rules and regulations relative thereto. We also recommended that the Council reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects, otherwise reprogram the same to those PPAs which will help build the disaster resilience of communities and will lessen the impact of deadly and costly natural and man-made hazards/disasters with the goal of promoting sustainable socioeconomic
growth. 

We recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat provide satisfactory explanation in the noted observations in the procurement process.

We recommended that the MDRRMO (i) maintain an organized inventory of DRRM equipment, supplies and materials for the efficient management of the same; and (ii) seek the assistance of GSO in the disposal of defective and unserviceable equipment, if repair of the same is not applicable anymore.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC was able to substantially implement the planned PPAs for disaster prevention and preparedness during the year with 52.33% utilization rate.
observations.reason: The implementation shall take place after MDC approval. The total amount of ₱658,360.33 is included in the MDRRMFIP intended for rehab of major river tributaries/ waterways.

observations.managementAction: The ESM Focal Person disclosed that commencement of construction of the drainage control system and piping (leachate management) for the SCRP was not implemented due to insufficiency of funds but these will be prioritized in the ensuing year. The Municipality continuously maintains its various projects of Residual Containment Area (RCA) and MRF at the controlled dumpsite area, continuously conducts Waste Analysis and Cha-racterizations Study (WACS) and other SWM awareness campaigns, and continuously enforces “Anti Littering” and “No to Plastic” policy.
observations.reason: From 30 MRFs (out of 70) in CY 2019, the Municipality have 24 MRFs only as of December 31, 2020. The others were destroyed by the typhoon. According to the MENRO, the establishment of MRFs per barangay were included in their respective SK Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor make a formal demand against Mantrade Development Corporation for the insufficient amount of liquidated damages amounting to ₱84,855.00 and require the BAC Chairman and members of the BAC to scrutinize the information indicated in the bidding documents particularly the period or dates being prescribed.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality submitted explanation for the noted issue. Partial payment amounting ₱44,200.00 was settled by the contractor under OR No. 0975419 dated December 16, 2019.
observations.reason: The claim of the Municipality was not prioritize due to COVID pandemic and other government transactions. Further, the filing of case under small claims against Mantrade Development Corporation is no longer taken into consideration since it is not economical and collection is not certain even if the agency win the case.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Municipal Accountant to a) exhaust all possible means to retrieve all records and reports to establish the validity and existence of the receivables, b) determine the liable officers who have been accessory to make the contracts with the defaulting debtors legal and binding and c) conduct a regular review, reconciliation, and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books; and

Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and Administrator a) exert efforts to know the whereabouts of the debtors and subsequently serve formal demand letters against them to enforce settlement of their unpaid loans; b) coordinate with the barangay officials of various Barangays for the collection of their counterpart for the purchase of dump truck; and c) initiate other legal actions to collect and recover the dormant receivables and request for writing off of those receivables no longer collectible in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant started its process of collating documents to support the write-off.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, they are yet to commence on processing the necessary requirements for the write-off of the dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that
the:

Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of the PPAs to maximize the utilization of 20% DF; and reassess the underlying problem and issues on the proposed projects before actual implementation;

Municipal Development Council with the assistance of the MPDO reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed developmental project. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and

Municipal Development Council and Budget Officer appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects.
observations.managementAction: According to concerned offices, project status were properly reviewed and monitored and reports were promptly submitted for verification.
observations.reason: On-going implementation of audit recommendations.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to submit justification on the noted deficiencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the HRMO require the employees without ATM cards to submit their application forms to the DBP as a requirement for subsequent issuance of ATM Cards to them;

the Management consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel and coordinate with the DBP officials and employees for the processing of the same; and

see to it that the cash advance granted shall not exceed the Maximum Cash Accountability authorized under her approved fidelity bond or else apply for an increased fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Automation of payroll system for Job Order personnel is still ongoing.
observations.reason: According to the Municipal Accountant, DBP is currently not issuing new ATMs for Job Order personnel, thus, payroll is made thru cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized the fund by trying their best to locate for suitable land as dumpsite and observe the criteria for sanitary landfill as required by Section 40 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipality, additional appropriation was already allotted and POW was already prepared. The Municipality is already on push through for the construction of Phase I for a cost of ₱6.5 million.
observations.reason: Construction of Phase I is for start-up at a cost of ₱6.5 million and is on the process of securing ECC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Assessor account for the additional twelve (12) land titles in custody of the Municipal Treasurer which were not included in the Municipal assessor’s records;

The Municipal Assessor, General Services Officer, and Treasurer coordinate and exert efforts in obtaining documents necessary for titling the parcels of land accounted in the name of the Municipality; and the

Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO and OIC-Municipal Assessor was able to submit a list of properties owned by the Municipality which disclosed that eleven (11) parcels of land were still untitled as of December 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Appropriation was included in the Supplemental Budget however was not utilized. The amount of ₱300,000.00 will be included in this year’s Supplemental Budget.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office furnished copies of PPEs to each department for their checking and classification in coordination with the MGSO. The collated data were summarized to establish the actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties. The Municipal Engineer was able to submit partial RPCLRN.
observations.reason: Voluminous workload and existence of long outstanding accounts that were still unidentified .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant compute for the actual amount of liquidated damages that should be demanded from the contractor. Moreover, scrutinize the information indicated in the documents particularly the period or dates being prescribed; OIC-MAgO submit the necessary supporting documents and ensure that future transactions be supported with complete documentation; Concerned officials and employees provide satisfactory explanation for the noted observations relative the non-operation of the portion of the projects and non-implementation in accordance with approved Program of Works and enforce the performance of the repair of the subjected portion of the projects from the contractor; and Municipal Engineer ensure that the quality of works and the implementation of the projects is in accordance with the approved design, plans, and specifications and ensure that the location of projects has no problems/issues relative the Land Ownership and/or Road Right-of-Way before the lot was used for the implementation of the project.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the lacking documents and recorded the cost under the Consultancy Services account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the: Municipal Development Council (MDC) with the assistance of the OIC-MPDO reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented PPAs of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed developmental project. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and MBO and MDC appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects. We also recommended that the: Municipal Engineer formulate policy guidelines that will aide in the effective and efficient coordination, monitoring, reporting and evaluation of the status of implementation of the PPAs for the optimal utilization of 20% DF; MDC with the assistance of the MPDO and MEO evaluate the status and condition of the implemented projects to assess whether completed projects are being used in accordance to the purpose for which it was constructed likewise evaluate if the projects implemented needs more funding to be more usable and beneficial to constituents; Municipal Mayor with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant make a formal demand against Maravilla Trading and Althaea Enterprises for the refund of the excess payment totaling ₱9,049.56 brought by the deduction of liquidated damages to project cost and the Municipal Treasurer make the necessary remittance to the BIR for the underpayment of taxes; Municipal Accountant make necessary adjustments for the understatement of asset account and income accounts; and MDC with the assistance of the MBO and MPDO, revert the savings accumulated for the implementation of various projects out of 20% DF and evaluate and compute further for the savings from recently completed projects and reprogram the same to more developmental projects to help achieve the desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to implement substantial number of projects funded under 20% DF. The Municipal Mayor requested that Maravilla Trading and Althaea Enterprises be spared from refunding liquidated damages and waiver to make the necessary adjustment for the affected asset and income accounts. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant were requested to effect the necessary adjustments accordingly.
observations.reason: Due to (i) existence of barangay programs and projects not entirely within the control of Municipality; (ii) projects that are still on the preparatory phase of project implementation; (iii) insufficiency of allotted funds for the selected projects; and (iv) lack of concrete plan for the implementation of the projects, the Municipal was still not able to optimally utilize the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer (a) strategically plan the implementation of mainstreamed projects of the LGU through proper planning and ensuring that needed materials for the completion of the projects will be provided on time; and (b) closely monitor the implementation of projects and ensure that projects will be accomplished on targeted date, except for unforeseen events, to uphold efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the operations of the Government.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality were able to complete the project as of December 31,2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Engineer make a justifiable explanation on the noted deficiency between the reported and the actual accomplishment of the project for the Construction of Three (3) Storey Evacuation Center. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor make a formal demand for the project against the Althaea Enterprises to perform corrective measures or refund the cost deficiency ₱139,766.37 attributed to the noted difference of 1.17% for the construction of evacuation center and the noted cracks and holes in the Riprapping of Tabang Creek.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was able to submit the approved variation order and caused the contractor to institute corrective actions. The Municipal Mayor was able to submit the justification on the noted deficiencies.
observations.reason: Documents were forwarded to the TAS for they evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count and technical valuation of the local road network to determine the current value of the road components to facilitate proper preparation of RPCLRN; Municipal Engineer prepare a schedule of the Local Roads indicating therein the cost of each Road Network Component such as (a) road lot, (b) road pavement, (c) drainage and slope protection structures, (d) and other miscellaneous structures to be submitted to the GSO and Accounting Office. Moreover, subsequent road projects of the Municipality should clearly indicate the separate cost of each road component for easy reference. The Engineering Office is also vested to determine the life of the Local Roads taking into consideration the (1) design, (2) type of users, and (3) volume of users of the roads. Such detail should also be included in the schedule of Local Roads to serve as basis for the depreciation of the Road Components to be applied by the Accounting Office; GSO prepare and maintain RLRN and RoadMap to account for the Local Roads. These records should be reconciled with the Registry of Public Infrastructures maintained by the Accounting Office; and Municipal Accountant prepare separate Subsidiary Ledgers for the determined cost of the Road Network Components, and thereafter provide for the depreciation of each Road Component based from the records to be prepared by the Engineering Office to establish proper valuation of the Road Network. We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Inventory Committee, in the course of a complete physical inventory of properties, account all determined unserviceable properties including the aforesaid Heavy Equipment to facilitate preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and other required supporting documentations for disposal. Thereafter, the Committee determine the salvage value of the properties based on property ledgers maintained by the Accounting Office, appraise the disposal value of the unserviceable properties through technical assessment by the Engineering Office, and dispose them pursuant to COA rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting Office furnished copies of PPEs to each department for their checking and classification in coordination with the MGSO. The collated data were summarized to establish the actual existence, ownership and condition of the properties. The Municipal Engineer was able to submit partial RPCLRN. Thereafter, the Inventory Committee, in the course of a complete physical inventory of properties, account all determined unserviceable properties including the aforesaid Heavy Equipment to facilitate preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and other required supporting documentations for disposal.
observations.reason: Voluminous workload and existence of long outstanding accounts that were still unidentified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Treasurer (i) formulate strategies to intensify collection of property taxes and raise awareness of tax payers, including postings of the notice of property tax dues, current and delinquent, to each of the seventy (70) Barangays of the Municipality thru the cooperation of the ABC President and the Barangay Officials; (ii) establish monitoring tool for the delinquent accounts which will aid in the easy generation of the report with information as to the aging of the delinquencies since the same cannot be generated using the iTax system; and (iii) exert additional effort to serve Notice of Delinquencies and Tax Due Bills to taxpayers with delinquent accounts in all the 70 barangays through the assistance of the ABC President, Punong Barangays and other Barangay officials. Municipal Assessor reconcile their data with the list provided by the MTO regarding properties suggested for restriction and/or exclusion; and require the Field Assessment and Appraisal Personnel to conduct regular tax mapping and property reassessment.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer’s Office conducted continuous Information and Education Campaign (IEC) on property tax in barangays, and issued 2,692 Tax Due Bills and 4,414 Notice of Delinquencies as of December 31,2019.
observations.reason: The iTax System lacks the feature to provide basic enumeration of listed properties; exclude delinquent property taxes attributable to restricted property taxes from the total amount of RPT delinquencies; and age delinquent property taxes. The overstatement of the reported delinquent RPT was due to inability to identify and exclude restricted RPT delinquencies; The Difference in exempted and restricted property records of Treasury and Assessment Office was due to limited resources to reconcile pertinent records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMC properly plan, prioritize and evaluate the PPAs to be funded out of the LDRRMF to attain the objective and purpose of the Fund by observing the rules and regulations relative thereto. We also recommended that the BAC and BAC Secretariat provide satisfactory explanation in the noted observations in the procurement process. We recommended that the MDRRMO (i) maintain an organized inventory of DRRM equipment, supplies and materials for the efficient management of the same; and (ii) seek the assistance of GSO in the disposal of defective and unserviceable equipment, if repair of the same is not applicable anymore.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMC was able to substantially implement the planned PPAs for disaster prevention and preparedness during the year with 60% utilization rate.
observations.reason: Considerable amount of the 5% LDRRMF were still unutilized due to insufficient funding and changes in the programmed PPAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: through the Lingang mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents through Information Education Campaign (IEC) on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the importance of their participation in the implementation of Anti-Littering and No to Plastic policies; through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of RA No. 9003 on waste segregation by placing trash bins with proper labels on the designated garbage collection area and other public places and collect only those wastes which are properly segregated and ensure that the existing MRF in 26 barangays are functional and the purpose of establishment of MRF is being served and provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining 44 barangays in establishing their own MRF; recommend to the SB possible measures that will enforce the full compliance of No Segregation, No Collection, Anti-Littering and No to Plastic policies of the constituents since the same will help in collecting raw materials to be used in the operation of composting facility; enforce the implementation of the program “Establishment of Composting Facility for Biodegradable Wastes” to attain the benefit of producing LGU’s own organic fertilizer and require the Municipal Engineer to construct the appropriate housing for the equipment to prevent them from exposure to deterioration; and see to it that the operation of Sanitary Landfill complied with Section 2 of Rule XIV of the IRR of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The ESM Focal Person disclosed that commencement of construction of the drainage control system and piping (leachate management) for the SCRP would then be prioritized the ensuing year. The Municipality was also able to compost 0.5 tons of biodegradable waste, encouraged participation of schools and promotion of SWM agenda to students under the “Search for Eco Friendly School” campaign, continuously maintenance of Residual Containment Area (RCA) and MRF at the controlled dumpsite area, continuously conduct of Waste Analysis and Characterizations Study (WACS) and other SWM awareness campaigns, and continuously enforce of “Anti Littering” and “No to Plastic” policy.
observations.reason: Only thirty (30) out of seventy (70) barangays of Municipality has established MRFs as required by RA No. 9003 due to insufficiency of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit; BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations in the procurement process thru alternative method under small value procurement; Members of the Inspection Committee and GSO properly accomplished the IAR by indicating the dates for the efficient and effective evaluation of the documents and transactions; and Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4 of P.D. 1445 and RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO was able to submit the lacking supporting documents and the liquidated damages were settled by the concerned contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC, TWG and BAC Secretariat submit satisfactory explanation on the noted observations in the procurement process relative to the acquisition of the customized rescue pickup and rescue dump truck; BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents for audit purposes; Municipal Mayor make a formal demand against Firecoach Trading for the insufficient amount of liquidated damages amounting to ₱87,100.00; Members of the Inspection Committee and GSO properly accomplished the IAR by indicating the dates for the efficient and effective evaluation of the documents and transactions; and Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer ensure that claims for the future transactions were supported with necessary documents before payments were made.
observations.managementAction: Liquidated damages were fully settled by Firecoach Trading

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: (i) Municipal Accountant to a) exhaust all possible means to retrieve all records and reports to establish the validity and existence of the receivables, b) determine the liable officers who have been accessory to make the contracts with the defaulting debtors legal and binding and c) conduct a regular review, reconciliation, and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books; and (ii) Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and Administrator a) exert efforts to know the whereabouts of the debtors and subsequently serve formal demand letters against them to enforce settlement of their unpaid loans; b) coordinate with the barangay officials of various Barangays for the collection of their counterpart for the purchase of dump truck; and c) initiate other legal actions to collect and recover the dormant receivables and request for writing off of those receivables no longer collectible in compliance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant expressed willingness to write off the dormant receivables.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of collating the documents to support the writeoff.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of the PPAs to maximize the utilization of 20% DF; and reassess the underlying problem and issues on the proposed projects before actual implementation; Municipal Development Council with the assistance of the MPDO reassess the necessity of pursuing the unimplemented projects of prior years, otherwise reprogram the same to a more needed developmental project. The Council should also properly plan and prioritize all activities to be implemented during the year; and Municipal Development Council and Budget Officer appropriate sufficient fund for the complete implementation of the identified projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality was able to implement substantial number of projects funded under 20% DF. The Municipal Mayor requested that Maravilla Trading and Althaea Enterprises be spared from refunding liquidated damages and waiver to make the necessary adjustment for the affected asset and income accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned contractor to refund the cost deficiency of ₱87,624.60 in the Construction of Slaughter House and the excess of contract amount above the COA estimates by a total of ₱125,132.79 due to misapplication of Indirect Cost and VAT.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to submit justification on the noted deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the contractor to rectify the noted deficiencies or refund the total cost deficiency.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor was able to submit justification on the noted deficiencies.
observations.reason: The submitted documents were forwarded to TAS for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: the HRMO require the employees without ATM cards to submit their application forms to the DBP as a requirement for subsequent issuance of ATM Cards to them; the Management consider the option of adopting the automation of payroll system for the payment of wages of JO personnel and coordinate with the DBP officials and employees for the processing of the same; and see to it that the cash advance granted shall not exceed the Maximum Cash Accountability authorized under her approved fidelity bond or else apply for an increased fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Automation of payroll system for Job Order personnel is still ongoing.
observations.reason: Only employees who were later admitted in service are paid thru cash advance thru payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Municipal government officials and employees optimally utilized the fund by trying their best to locate for suitable land as dumpsite and observe the criteria for sanitary landfill as required by Section 40 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: An amount of ₱7 million under the 20% Development Fund for the establishment of the Sanitary Land Fill was appropriated thru Sangguniang Bayan Resolution No. 150-2019 dated May 23, 2019.
observations.reason: Allotted funds were not utilized due to limited information on the exact reference points, coordinates and ground elevation of the proposed location.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Municipal Assessor account for the additional twelve (12) land titles in custody of the Municipal Treasurer which were not included in the Municipal assessor’s records; The Municipal Assessor, General Services Officer, and Treasurer coordinate and exert efforts in obtaining documents necessary for titling the parcels of land accounted in the name of the Municipality; and the Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality to preserve legal rights over the real properties and to avoid exposure to third party claims.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO and OIC-Municipal Assessor was able to submit a list of properties owned by the Municipality which disclosed that nine (9) parcels of land were still untitled as of December 31,2019. The Municipal Budget Officer annually appropriate funds on a per parcel basis to ensure that the Municipality’s budget can accommodate the survey and titling of each land in the name of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to lack of available records and manpower to secure relevant documents, titling of the lots was still not effected.

Un-acted unserviceable heavy equipment We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Inventory Committee, in the course of a complete physical inventory of properties, account all determined unserviceable properties including the aforesaid Heavy Equipment to facilitate preparation of Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and other required supporting documentations for disposal. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Individual Accountable Officers granted with cash advances (a) strictly submit the liquidation documents upon arrival/return to their official travel, (b) return the excess cash advances, if any, simultaneously with the liquidation report, and (c) in case of cancelled travel, AOs immediately and fully account and refund the cash advance and submit valid justification for not pursuing the proposed travel; The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer closely monitor the liquidation of cash advances and issue demand letters to concerned officials and employees with cash advances that remained unsettled for more than thirty (30) days; and Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salaries due to Officials and Employees concerned with overdue unliquidated cash advances to prevent misappropriation of public funds, overstatement of Due from Officers and Employees account and understatement of affected expense accounts for a given period.
observations.managementAction: 2017 AAR Pages 37-40 The Accounting Office properly monitors maturity of the CAs and sends appropriate demand letters for maturing and/or overdue accounts, substantially reducing the amount of untimely liquidations of the AOs and prevented granting of additional CAs to untimely liquidating AOs and prevented granting of additional CAs to AOs with unsettled accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: a) We recommended that the: Municipal Treasurer (i) formulate strategies to intensify collection of property taxes and raise awareness of tax payers, including individual postings of the notice of property tax dues, current and delinquent, to each of the seventy (70) Barangays of the Municipality thru the cooperation of the ABC President and the Barangay Officials; (ii) establish monitoring tool for the delinquent accounts which will aid in the easy generation of the report with information as to the aging of the delinquencies since the same cannot be generated using the iTax system; and (iii) exert additional effort to serve Notice of Delinquencies and Tax Due Bills to taxpayers with delinquent accounts in all the 70 barangays through the assistance of the ABC President, Punong Barangays and other Barangay officials. b) Municipal Assessor reconcile their data with the list provided by the MTO regarding properties suggested for restriction and/or exclusion; and require the Field Assessment and Appraisal Personnel to conduct regular tax mapping and property reassessment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Social Worker and Development Officer (MSWDO) submit the lacking documents to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made and ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (P.D.) 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012; and (b) MSWDO provide justification or computation to serve as a basis for the allocation of funds for viand of the children in different Day Care Centers and provide the documents as proof to where the ending balance of the 5th cycle amounting ₱149,009.81 was expended or if the same has been refunded.
observations.managementAction: 2017 AAR Pages 43-45 Submission of the lacking documents identified by the Audit Team upon verification of vouchers and its supporting documents.

observations.managementAction: 2017 AAR Pages 46 Submission of the lacking documents identified by the Audit Team upon verification of vouchers and its supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: (a) Municipal Accountant, Bids and Awards (BAC) Chairman and the Municipal Engineer submit the lacking documents within five days to support the propriety and validity of the disbursement made to avoid suspension and/or disallowance in audit; (b) Municipal Accountant and Treasurer ensure that future claims be supported with complete documentation as required by Section 4(6) of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; (c) Management to provide explanation why the retained 3% of the fund transfer for administrative cost was not considered in the budgeting and costing of the project; and (d) BAC Chairman ensure compliance with prescribed period in the procurement process under the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR.
